operating_state: RTX generated $67.1 billion consolidated revenue in 2022, up 4% driven by commercial aerospace recovery with Collins and Pratt & Whitney achieving double-digit organic growth from increased aftermarket sales and aircraft utilization. Defense segments experienced organic declines due to supply chain constraints and lower production volumes. Operating cash flows remained stable at $6.8 billion despite higher tax payments from research expenditure capitalization requirements.
cost_structure: Consolidated operating profit increased to $5.4 billion with margins improving to 8.1% from 7.7%, driven by commercial aerospace aftermarket recovery and reduced acquisition accounting adjustments. Commercial segments achieved significant margin expansion while defense segments experienced compression due to unfavorable program mix and supply chain impacts. Capital expenditures increased primarily for Pratt & Whitney production facility investments.
material_events: RTX reversed $1.3 billion of Russian backlog in Q1 2022 due to global sanctions, recognizing $296 million in associated charges. The company executed $2.8 billion in share repurchases and paid $3.1 billion in dividends. Management announced organizational restructuring from four to three business segments consolidating defense operations into a single Raytheon segment for H2 2023 implementation.
macro_exposures: Global supply chain disruptions continue causing material shortages, delivery delays, and price increases across raw materials and microelectronics, while labor market constraints persist for specialized engineering and security clearance personnel. Geopolitical tensions escalated with Russian sanctions impacting operations and potential Chinese sanctions related to Taiwan military sales. Climate-related physical risks including extreme weather events pose increased threats to facilities and supply chains.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued commercial aerospace recovery driving growth through increased aftermarket demand and OEM production, though timing of full capacity restoration remains uncertain. The company maintains $175 billion total backlog providing strong foundation. Strategic focus remains on digital transformation initiatives, sustainable aviation technology investments, and structural cost reductions including facility optimization and advanced manufacturing capabilities.
uncertainty_profile: RTX faces ongoing DOJ investigation into defense contract pricing matters with $147 million accrual established, while maintaining exposure to annual acquisition accounting adjustments from merger intangibles. Two Collins reporting units have fair values exceeding book values by only 15-17% representing $9.5 billion combined goodwill exposure. Long-term contract accounting involves significant estimation uncertainty around labor productivity and material costs, while pension obligations remain sensitive to discount rate assumptions.
